title: Allow filtering by image digest in REST API
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

/kind feature

**Description**

The [API endpoint](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/_static/api.html#tag/images/operation/ImageListLibpod) `GET /libpod/images/json` allows filtering by image ID using the query parameter 
```
GET /libpod/images/json?filters={"id":["5d53c..."]}`
```

I propose adding a filter for `digest` in addition to `id`. For example:
```
GET /libpod/images/json?filters={"digest":["sha256:81bf1d..."]}
```

A use-case for this is when scanning images; the user/agent interested in the scan may know, for example, the content-addressable image digest but not the podman image ID. We may want to scan the image regardless of where it is located, whether in podman's store, containerd's store, or docker's store. This is the case in [trivy](https://github.com/aquasecurity/trivy), which will scan images in any of those locations. Without the ability to search by digest, trivy has to be run again after discerning the Image ID from podman.

As a more general benefit, it is always useful to find content-addressable things by a digest of their contents.

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I would be fine with accepting a PR for this, are you interested in implementing this?
@rhatdan It was pretty trivial to implement, but what's expected in terms of tests?
Greate a coule of images, and then filter by id and show that it only shows the one that matches.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

@rhatdan The feature is simple but I haven't been able to write tests for it. I'm not able to work on it at the moment. Relevant diff is here if anyone wants to take over: https://github.com/containers/podman/compare/1c8b40d097a21d028896b913bcee238487a8b7d7...pmengelbert:podman:feat/digest_filter?expand=1

Otherwise I may return to this when time permits
